# Review a proposal

!!! info "About this walkthrough"
    **Estimated time:** 2 minutes
    **Tags:** proposals, review, git-ops

Open a pending proposal, read the config diff and metric delta, then decide whether to open a PR or reject.

## Step 1 — Open the Proposals page. Every winning study config…

![Open the Proposals page. Every winning study config and every manually-authored proposal lands here. Filter chips at the top scope by status, source, and cluster — start with 'All' to see everything.](../../assets/guides/02_review_a_proposal/01-proposals-list.png)

## Step 2 — Click the 'pending' chip to narrow to proposals…

![Click the 'pending' chip to narrow to proposals that haven't been opened as a PR or rejected yet. The URL updates with ?status=pending so the filter survives page reloads and is sharable.](../../assets/guides/02_review_a_proposal/02-proposals-pending-filter.png)

## Step 3 — Click a proposal row to open its detail…

![Click a proposal row to open its detail page. You'll see the source (study or manual), the cluster + template it targets, the proposed config changes, and the metric delta (when the proposal came from a completed study).](../../assets/guides/02_review_a_proposal/03-proposal-detail.png)

## Step 4 — The Config diff panel is the load-bearing review…

![The Config diff panel is the load-bearing review surface. Each row is one parameter key being changed: title.boost from 1.0 → 2.5, description.boost from 1.0 → 0.8. The 'FROM' column shows the cluster's current value; 'TO' shows what the PR will write. (Manual proposals don't carry a baseline metric — those only ship from completed studies.)](../../assets/guides/02_review_a_proposal/04-config-diff.png)

## Step 5 — If the proposal isn't right — wrong cluster,…

![If the proposal isn't right — wrong cluster, regressed a query class the metric didn't capture, off-target for the current sprint — click 'Reject'. Provide a brief reason; rejected proposals stay in the queue but are filtered out by default. Alternatively, click 'Open PR' (top right of the detail page) to enqueue the GitHub PR worker.](../../assets/guides/02_review_a_proposal/05-reject-dialog.png)
